skill.name: cover-letter-drafter
skill.description: Generates professional cover letters for journal submissions, academic job applications, and fellowship applications using customizable templates.
skill.summary: AI-assisted cover letter generator for academic and medical professionals.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 61 potential issues including backtick execution, weak cryptographic algorithms, hardcoded URLs, system reconnaissance, and path traversal. After expert review, all 61 static findings are confirmed FALSE POSITIVES: markdown backticks were misidentified as shell execution, variable names containing 'key' triggered cryptographic flags, documentation URLs were flagged as hardcoded, and security checklist items about preventing path traversal were misread as actual traversal attempts. The skill is a straightforward template-based cover letter generator using only Python standard library with no network access, no credential handling, and no shell command execution. The only operational risk is the intended file write operation for saving generated letters.

low_findings.title: File Write to User-Specified Path Without Workspace Validation
low_findings.description: The script writes output via open(args.output, 'w') at line 205 to a user-provided file path without validating the path is within the intended workspace directory. While this is the skill's intended functionality, an arbitrary output path could write outside expected boundaries if the execution environment does not sandbox file access.

dangerous_patterns.title: User-Controlled File Write Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The --output argument accepts an arbitrary file path from the user and writes JSON output to that location without restricting the path to a designated workspace directory.

content.user_title: Draft Academic Cover Letters for Journals and Jobs
content.value_statement: Writing professional cover letters for academic submissions is time-consuming and requires careful attention to format and tone. This skill generates tailored cover letters for journal submissions, job applications, and fellowship applications using structured templates and user-provided details, saving researchers time while maintaining professional standards.
actual_capabilities: Generate journal submission cover letters with manuscript title and significance statements
Create job application cover letters for academic and medical positions
Produce fellowship application letters highlighting research achievements
Accept comma-separated key points for flexible content customization
Output structured JSON with cover letter text and word count metadata
Save generated letters to a user-specified output file
limitations: Template-based output requires manual review and personalization before submission
Limited to three letter types: journal submission, job application, and fellowship
Does not access external databases or verify recipient names and institution details
Requires users to provide all key points; cannot infer achievements from context

use_cases.title: Journal Manuscript Submission
use_cases.description: A researcher preparing to submit a manuscript generates a professional cover letter addressed to the journal editor, highlighting the study significance and key findings.

use_cases.title: Academic Job Application
use_cases.description: A postdoctoral researcher applying for a faculty position creates a tailored cover letter emphasizing their experience, publications, and fit for the department.

use_cases.title: Fellowship Application
use_cases.description: An early-career investigator applying for a research fellowship drafts a letter presenting their research excellence and career goals to the funding committee.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Journal Cover Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cover letter for submitting my manuscript titled "[title]" to [journal name]. The key points to highlight are: [point 1], [point 2], [point 3]. My name is [author name].
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user needs a simple journal submission cover letter

prompt_templates.title: Academic Job Application Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a job application cover letter for the [position title] position at [institution]. My key qualifications are: [qualification 1], [qualification 2], [qualification 3]. My name is [applicant name].
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher applying for an academic faculty or staff position

prompt_templates.title: Fellowship Application Letter
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a cover letter for my [fellowship name] fellowship application at [institution]. My key research achievements are: [achievement 1], [achievement 2]. My name is [applicant name].

prompt_templates.title: Custom Script Execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the cover letter drafter script with these parameters: purpose=[journal|job|fellowship], recipient="[institution name]", key-points="[point 1], [point 2]", author="[name]", output="[file path]".
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced user needs precise control over script parameters and output location

output_examples.input: Generate a journal cover letter for my manuscript on cancer immunotherapy research submitted to Nature Medicine
output_examples.output:
Dear Editor,

I am pleased to submit our manuscript titled "Novel Immunotherapy Approaches in Cancer Treatment" for consideration for publication in Nature Medicine.

• First demonstration of combined checkpoint inhibition in resistant tumors
• Clinical relevance for patients who have failed standard therapies

This work represents a major advance in the field. We believe it aligns with the scope and readership of your journal.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,
Dr. Smith

output_examples.input: Write a cover letter for an Assistant Professor position at Harvard Medical School highlighting my experience and publications
output_examples.output:
Dear Hiring Committee,

I am writing to express my strong interest in the Assistant Professor position at Harvard Medical School.

• 10 years of research experience in molecular biology
• Published 20 peer-reviewed papers in high-impact journals
• Secured over 2M in grant funding as Principal Investigator

I am excited about the opportunity to contribute to your team.

Thank you for your consideration.

Sincerely,
Dr. Jones

best_practices: Provide specific, measurable key points that highlight your most relevant and distinctive achievements
Always review and personalize the generated letter before sending it to a recipient
Verify all recipient names, institution details, and position titles are accurate before submission
anti_patterns: Submitting the generated letter without reviewing and personalizing the template content
Using vague or generic key points that do not distinguish your application from other candidates
Expecting the skill to automatically know your publication history, credentials, or personal background details

faq.question: What types of cover letters can this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates cover letters for three purposes: journal manuscript submissions, academic job applications, and fellowship applications.

faq.question: Do I need to install any Python packages to use this skill?
faq.answer: No additional packages are required. The script uses only Python standard library modules such as argparse, json, and typing.

faq.question: Can I customize the letter templates to match my style?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit the TEMPLATES dictionary in scripts/main.py to modify the letter structure, tone, and formatting to suit your preferences.

faq.question: How do I provide the key points I want highlighted in the letter?
faq.answer: Use the --key-points parameter with comma-separated values, for example: --key-points "10 years experience, Published 20 papers, Grant funding".

faq.question: Can I save the generated cover letter to a file instead of printing to the console?
faq.answer: Yes, use the --output or -o parameter to specify a file path. The cover letter will be saved as a JSON file at that location.

faq.question: What happens if I forget to provide a required parameter?
faq.answer: The script displays a clear error message indicating which required parameter is missing and exits. For example, omitting --title for a journal letter shows: Error: --title required for journal cover letters.
